DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF MIRNA VASQUEZ' MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS OF BEVERLY HARRISON AND RICHARD HARRISON AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,740.00. THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THERE IS NO BASIS FOR THE PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST THAT THE SUBJECT DEPOSITIONS TAKE PLACE IN CALIFORNIA. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL HAS SET REMOTE DEPOSITIONS IN THIS CASE AND THESE DEPOSITIONS ARE NO DIFFERENT. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL HAS PRESENTED REASONABLE EXPLANATIONS FOR THE CURRENT SCHEDULING DELAYS AND OFFERED MULTIPLE ALTERNATIVE DATES FOR THE DEPOSITIONS TO TAKE PLACE. IF THE DEFENDANTS DO NOT APPEAR ON THE DATE(S) OFFERED, PLAINTIFF MAY RENEW THIS MOTION. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 VIA EMAIL TO NOAH@NDLLEGAL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: NOAH LEBOWITZ, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions Of Beverly Harrison And Richard Harrison And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,740.00; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Sandeep G. Agarwal, Esq.
Off Calendar Dec-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MIRNA VASQUEZ' Motion To Compel Depositions Of Beverly Harrison And Richard Harrison And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,740.00

The motion is DENIED without prejudice. There is no basis for the Plaintiff's request that the subject depositions take place in California. Plaintiff's counsel has set remote depositions in this case and these depositions are no different. Defendant's counsel has presented reasonable explanations for the current scheduling delays and offered multiple alternative dates for the depositions to take place. If the Defendants do not appear on the date(s) offered, Plaintiff may renew this motion. The request for sanctions is DENIED.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to noah@ndllegal.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Noah Lebowitz, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
